beats.text: The teen produces a bong shaped like a dragon from inside a milk crate. You are too far in to turn back.

beats.speaker: Quikstop Teen
beats.text: Okay okay okay you first, you FIRST, dude.

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: *cough* — oh god. Oh GOD. That is — is that supposed to —

beats.speaker: Quikstop Teen
beats.text: Bro bro bro so my band? We're called Suburban Dads. IRONICALLY. Like. Our drummer's dad left and mine is a cop so —

beats.speaker: Quikstop Teen
beats.text: — and the SINGER is a GIRL, which is WILD, and our EP drops on Bandcamp, dude, BANDCAMP, nobody uses Bandcamp, that's the POINT —

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: I think my legs are asleep. I think my SOUL is asleep.
Footsteps. A woman's voice. 'KYLE! Kyle are you back here with someone's DAD?'

beats.speaker: Teen's Mom
beats.text: Are you SERIOUSLY smoking with my SEVENTEEN YEAR OLD SON? In an ALLEY?

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: Ma'am I — I can explain — I cannot explain —

beats.speaker: Teen's Mom
beats.text: You better START explaining before I find a COP, Khakis.

beats.speaker: Teen's Mom
beats.text: ...this is fifty dollars. This doesn't FIX anything. But it is fifty dollars.
She pockets it. Stomps off muttering about her book club, Kyle's report card, and 'the MORAL DECLINE of this CUL-DE-SAC.'

beats.speaker: Dad
beats.text: I have to — I have a thing — GOODBYE —
You panic-walk out of the alley. Arms stiff. Not running. Definitely not running. A man power-walking from justice.
Behind you: 'I'M CALLING THE POLICE, KHAKIS! I GOT YOUR LICENSE PLATE!' She did not get your license plate. Probably.
